title: Palindrome Linked List
description: Given a singly linked list, determine if it is a palindrome. A linked list is considered a palindrome if its elements are the same when read forward and backward.

examples.explanation: The elements are the same when read from both directions: 1, 2, 2, 1.
The elements read forward are 1, 2, 3, 4 which do not match their reverse 4, 3, 2, 1.
The elements are the same when read from both directions: 1, 2, 1.

constraints: The linked list has at most 10^5 nodes.
Each node's value is an integer within the range of [-10^5, 10^5].
hints: Consider using a two-pointer approach to find the middle of the linked list.
You may need to reverse the second half of the linked list for comparison.
Make sure to handle the case when the linked list has an odd number of elements.
Using a stack can help in comparing the two halves of the list.
